title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Philip and Mary (referred to as Polly) engage in a tense argument regarding their relationship and the ongoing murder investigation of Mrs. Aghel. Mary expresses a possessive desire for Philip, but he dismisses her, noting that they have run out of things to say. Philip reveals that he suspects Tina is lying about her whereabouts on the night of the murder. He shares gossip from a maid, Mrs. Nalcoat, stating that her son, Sir Cyril, went to the police because he saw something outside the house on the night Mrs. Aghel was killed. Philip theorizes that Tina may have witnessed or heard something and intends to use a method of questioning—asking yes or no questions—to uncover the truth.

The conversation turns to the emotional toll the investigation is taking on others, including Hester, Corston, Leo, and Gwendra. Philip becomes angry when Mary shows indifference toward these people. After Mary leaves the room, Philip reflects on his marriage, feeling a disconnect between the gentle girl he married and the cold, calculating woman Mary has become. He begins to wonder if her emotions are merely manipulations to achieve her goals.

Later, Philip is interrupted by Gwendolyn Wain, who brings him a newspaper on behalf of Leo. During their brief interaction, Philip makes a comment about "newlyweds," which causes Gwendolyn to tremble. He then pointedly asks her when she and Leo plan to marry.
